latest.title: New Website
latest.content:
1 Jul 2026 08:00
RNS Number : 5642K
dotDigital Group plc
01 July 2026
1 July 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
New Website
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, announces the launch of its new website alongside a refresh of the Dotdigital commercial brand.
The new corporate website is accessible at
https://dotdigital.com/
. Investor relations and AIM Rule 26 information will now be accessible at
investors.dotdigital.com
.
The website launch coincides with the Group's commercial rebrand and reflects Dotdigital's evolution into an all-in-one customer experience and data platform, supported by sustained investment in AI, data and personalisation and the acquisitions of Fresh Relevance, Social Snowball and Alia Software. Following a global, digital launch today, the new branding and product roadmap will be presented at
Dotdigital Summit
on 2 July, the Group's flagship customer event.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented:
"
Our new brand and website are a clear expression of our market positioning and the value we deliver to more than 9,800 customers globally. We remain focused on maintaining the pace of product innovation, with further enhancements to come as we continue to expand the capability of the platform through our AI-focused roadmap."
Contacts
Dotdigital Group plc
+44 (0)20 3953 3072
Milan Patel, CEO
investorrelations@dotdigital.com
Tom Mullan, CFO
Alma Strategic Communications
+44 (0)20 3405 0210
Hilary Buchanan
dotdigital@almastrategic.com
David Ison
Will Merison
Canaccord Genuity (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams, Corporate Finance
Sunila de Silva, Equity Capital Markets
Singer Capital Markets (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7496 3000
Shaun Dobson
Jen Boorer
About Dotdigital
Dotdigital Group plc (AIM: DOTD) is a leading provider of customer experience and data platform technology to marketing professionals. Dotdigital's CXDP combines the power of automation, data and AI to help businesses deliver hyper-relevant customer experiences at scale. With Dotdigital, marketing teams can unify and enrich their customer data, identify valuable customer segments, and deliver personalised cross-channel customer journeys that result in engagements, conversions, and loyalty.
Founded in 1999, Dotdigital is headquartered in London with offices in Manchester, Southampton, New York, Melbourne, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Warsaw and Cape Town. Dotdigital's solutions empower over 9,800 brands across 150 countries.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Dotdigital delivers a cloud‑based marketing automation platform that lets brands send emails, SMS, push notifications and build web experiences all from one interface. The software stitches together customer data, triggers, and analytics so marketers can create personalised campaigns without heavy IT involvement.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global marketing‑tech market is expanding into the $200‑$300 billion range, with small‑to‑mid‑size businesses increasingly outsourcing digital campaigns. Dotdigital’s low‑code platform gives these customers a cost‑effective alternative to custom development, giving the company a defensible niche in a crowded space.
company_30s.current_state: Dotdigital is in a rebuild phase: it recently added a small competitor (Alia), launched a refreshed website, and hired a new chief revenue officer. The stock trades around 45 p, well below the price the company’s valuation would suggest, indicating a potential bargain but also the volatility typical of a small‑cap turnaround.

rns_narrative: Leadership and financial updates are the main story: a new CRO, share‑holding changes, and a series of earnings releases are keeping the board in the spotlight.
Product and marketing moves form the second thread: the acquisition of Alia expands the platform’s capabilities, while a new website signals an aggressive brand‑refresh.
Investor engagement is the third theme: the company is actively communicating results and updates, yet its share price remains highly sensitive to any news.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious optimism – traders are waiting for a clear sign that the turnaround is working.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the Alia acquisition and the new CRO as evidence that sales momentum will return.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that recent earnings have been flat, cash burn is still high, and the market is still skeptical about the company’s ability to scale.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat for the past month; no sharp spikes in chatter, which is typical for a stock that hasn’t had a catalyst yet.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, meaning any future news is likely to generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Q3 earnings release – a positive surprise would validate the turnaround narrative.
Full integration of Alia’s features – if the platform can show measurable lift in campaign performance, it will drive demand.
New partnership announcements – a deal with a large agency or retailer would add credibility.
Volume spike – a jump of 2‑3× average daily volume often precedes a bounce in small caps.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm the thesis: (1) trading above the 50‑p support level with sustained volume; (2) a positive earnings beat; (3) a clear statement from the CRO about revenue targets.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early recovery stage of a deep crash. A strong catalyst is required before mean reversion can take off; otherwise the price may stay flat or decline further.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock recovers to roughly the midpoint between its current 45 p level and the last all‑time high, the upside is about 75 %. A deep crash of 70‑90 % historically produces large rebounds, and the recent acquisition and leadership changes add a credible catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Weak earnings and ongoing cash burn keep downside real. If the next earnings miss, the price could fall to 25 p or lower, wiping out the current position. There is no clear delisting risk yet, but the company remains vulnerable to a prolonged slump.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: ~55 % if the price falls to 25 p. Potential gain: ~75 % if it reaches 80 p. The asymmetry favors the upside, but the risk of a deeper slide remains.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter near the current 45 p level with a 10 % portfolio allocation. Place a stop 10 p below the entry (35 p). If the price climbs to 50 p, consider tightening the stop to 45 p and taking 30 % of the position for profit. If it reaches 70 p, lock in the remaining 70 % and exit.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the Q3 earnings call. A positive surprise or a clear roadmap from the CRO will be the trigger. Also watch for a volume spike above 2× the 30‑day average – that often precedes a breakout.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings have been announced.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Dotdigital’s deep discount, fresh acquisition, and new CRO create a contrarian play that could deliver 70‑80 % upside if Q3 results validate the turnaround, but weak earnings keep the downside realistic.

Historical data shows 1 rallies averaging 90% upside. Current position: +17.3%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
